location.office_time_required: hybrid — combines time in offices/workshops and time working from home

level_signals: Owns end-to-end materials execution for autonomous vehicle programs
Acts as primary local point of contact for suppliers and escalation management
Cross-functional coordination across Engineering, Supply Chain, Operations, and external partners
No explicit years of experience stated

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience bringing newly developed products to scale through supplier and partner relationships
Strong project or program management experience with the ability to influence across teams
Experience coordinating materials procurement, receiving, storage, and shipping
Professional proficiency in English and Japanese (written and verbal)
Knowledge of Japanese logistics, import/export regulations, and local business etiquette
Ability to perform cost analysis and support negotiations
Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills
Comfortable working in an Agile environment with aggressive timelines

scope.surface: End-to-end materials execution for Wayve's autonomous vehicle and hardware programs in Japan
primary_metrics_inferred: On-time materials procurement and readiness aligned to program milestones
CTB (Clear to Build) visibility with reduced last-minute procurement escalations
Accurate material receiving, storage, and inventory tracking
Reliable and compliant outbound shipping execution
Improved cost visibility and supplier execution
core_responsibilities: Own and manage materials programs enabling Wayve's autonomous vehicle and hardware development in Japan
Drive execution across procurement, receiving, storage, and shipping to meet aggressive program milestones
Own materials procurement activities in coordination with sourcing and purchasing teams
Drive and manage the CTB (Clear to Build) process with suppliers, contract manufacturers, workshop teams, and internal stakeholders
Work closely with Engineering to onboard, set up, and manage suppliers and partners within the Japanese market
Coordinate and oversee material receiving in Japan, ensuring accuracy, traceability, and proper documentation
Manage secure storage of high-value, long-lead-time materials, including inventory tracking and access controls
Coordinate outbound shipping of materials to workshops, vehicle upfitting locations, or other Wayve sites
Act as a central point of coordination across Engineering, Supply Chain, Operations, and external partners
Clearly communicate risks, tradeoffs, and decisions to both working-level teams and leadership

green_flags: Clear and specific scope — Japan-based materials execution for AV hardware programs
Well-defined success metrics explicitly listed in JD
Hybrid working policy with flexible scheduling (core hours model)
Inclusive hiring statement and DEI commitment
Encourages applications from candidates who don't meet every requirement
red_flags: Professional proficiency in both English and Japanese is a hard requirement — eliminates most non-Japanese market candidates
No explicit years of experience stated, making seniority level ambiguous
Visa sponsorship not mentioned — unclear for international candidates
Fast-paced/aggressive timelines language signals high execution pressure

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from scope of responsibilities and ownership level; no explicit level or years stated)
scope.business_model (inferred from company description and product context)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (directly quoted from 'Success Metrics' section; labeled inferred as they are stated goals not historical metrics)
ambiguities: Role title in identity fields is 'Material Program Manager' (singular) but JD heading and body use 'Materials Program Manager' (plural) — used verbatim identity field as instructed.
